meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: A Freelancer's Guide to Faster Shipping
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'can't debug code' wall. A beginner-friendly checklist for freelancers to master AI-assisted debugging using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at red error logs. Use AI-driven debugging to isolate root causes, explain stack traces, and generate automated test cases. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for CLI-based iteration, you transform complex bugs into solved tickets in minutes, maintaining your velocity and client satisfaction.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your terminal is bleeding red. You’ve refactored the function three times, but the logic remains broken. This isn't just a bug; it's a bottleneck that threatens your reputation and your bottom line. We have all been in that 3 AM dark room, staring at code that refuses to cooperate.

But the era of manual, line-by-line detective work is over. By leveraging AI-native development tools, you can externalize your cognitive load to high-performance models that see patterns invisible to the tired human eye. It is time to stop fighting the compiler and start orchestrating your success with precision-engineered AI workflows.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure State
steps.description: Before fixing, define the exact inputs that trigger the crash to ensure the AI analyzes the right scope.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following stack trace and identify the exact line of code causing the memory leak or logic error.

steps.title: Map Dependencies
steps.description: Understand how the failing module interacts with the rest of your codebase to prevent regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dependency map for this module and highlight potential side effects of modifying the current logic.

steps.title: Generate a Targeted Fix
steps.description: Request a surgical patch rather than a rewrite to keep your codebase clean and readable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a minimal code fix that resolves this bug without breaking existing API contracts.

steps.title: Automate Verification
steps.description: Write a unit test that fails without the fix and passes with it to ensure long-term stability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Jest test case that recreates this bug and validates the proposed fix.

steps.title: Refactor for Resilience
steps.description: Review the code with AI to proactively identify future failure points in the same module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this code for potential edge cases and suggest defensive programming improvements.

pro_tips: Always include your environment context (Node version, OS, framework) in your first prompt to avoid irrelevant suggestions.
Use the 'Chain of Thought' technique: ask the AI to explain its reasoning before proposing the code solution.
If the AI suggests a breaking change, ask it to write a migration script to handle the transition automatically.
Store your best AI-debugging prompts in a local snippet manager like Raycast or Alfred for instant recall.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'play devil's advocate' and find reasons why your current fix might fail in production.
resources: The Pragmatic Programmer's Guide to Debugging: https://pragprog.com
content.next_steps: Implement this workflow in your next client project, document the time saved, and begin building a library of custom prompts tailored to your specific tech stack.
content.conversion_block: Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
